user_message: Can you help me triage this? The customer (Fourth Coffee) is asking about security posture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 10:58 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 16:22 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 16:50 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Fourth Coffee

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Fourth Coffee)

[00:01:30] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into security posture.

[00:07:00] Priya (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:10:00] Priya (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Priya (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:23:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:27:00] Priya (Fourth Coffee): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-H trap (Skips 'open items from last meeting'); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-H failure mode (Skips 'open items from last meeting')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-H -- Skips 'open items from last meeting'
